assertions.expectation: Explains this is related to the interaction between maxSurge, drain order, and pod scheduling
assertions.reason: The response clearly explains the interaction between GKE's default upgrade parallelism (~20 nodes simultaneously) outpacing pod startup times, and provides specific maxSurge/maxUnavailable configuration examples

assertions.expectation: Notes that GKE cordons nodes before draining them — pods should prefer scheduling onto uncordoned (upgraded) nodes
assertions.reason: The response explicitly mentions that nodes get cordoned before draining in the root cause analysis sequence, and explains how this affects pod scheduling

assertions.expectation: Identifies possible cause: too many nodes being drained simultaneously (high maxSurge) before enough upgraded nodes exist
assertions.reason: The response clearly identifies GKE's default upgrade parallelism (~20 nodes simultaneously) as the root cause and explains how this outpaces pod startup times

assertions.expectation: Recommends reducing maxSurge to 1 to ensure upgraded nodes are available before draining the next batch
assertions.reason: The response provides specific gcloud commands to set max-surge-upgrade to 1 and explains this creates sequential behavior for stable landing spots

assertions.expectation: Suggests using pod anti-affinity or node affinity to prefer already-upgraded nodes
assertions.reason: The response mentions pod anti-affinity for spreading across nodes and provides YAML examples with topology spread constraints

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if any taints or tolerations are causing pods to land on the wrong nodes
assertions.reason: The response does not discuss taints or tolerations as a potential cause of the scheduling issues

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl commands to check node cordoned status and pod scheduling events
assertions.reason: The response provides specific kubectl commands to monitor pod scheduling events, node status, and evictions in the monitoring section

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting maxUnavailable=0 to ensure old nodes aren't removed until pods are safely rescheduled
assertions.reason: The response explicitly recommends max-unavailable-upgrade 0 in the gcloud command and explains this in the prevention section

assertions.expectation: May mention GKE's autoscaled blue-green upgrade strategy as an alternative that avoids this issue by cordoning the entire old pool before migrating workloads to new nodes
assertions.reason: The response dedicates a full section to blue-green upgrade strategy, provides gcloud commands to enable it, and explains how it eliminates the musical chairs problem
